body.title: DickSimnel: double-close bug — already-closed ticket escalates instead of succeeds
body.result: _post_result: show() check before escalate when close returns None; 2 tests (closed+done both succeed)
body.intention: I intend that a ticket the model closes via Bash tool during the ToolLoop is treated as a success by _post_result(), not escalated.
body.description:
**Problem:** The sprint-ticket skill instructs DickSimnel to call `cc_queue.py close <id> '...'` via Bash tool (step 11). When that succeeds, the model writes 'DONE: ...'. Then _post_result() calls cc_queue.py close AGAIN on the already-closed ticket. cc_queue returns None (ticket not found or already closed) → _post_result() treats None as close failure → escalates. Result: ticket closed AND escalated.

**What to build:** In `_post_result()`, before calling cc_queue close, check if the ticket is already closed. If `cc_queue show <id>` returns status in ('done', 'closed'), treat as success and increment _tickets_processed rather than escalating.

Alternatively: make cc_queue.py close idempotent (already-closed returns success JSON instead of None/error).

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (_post_result method), tests/inference/test_dicksimnel.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Fix _post_result() double-close handling only; do not change ToolLoop or other methods
**Completion criteria:** Test shows: ticket already in done/closed status → _post_result() increments _tickets_processed, does NOT call _escalate_ticket(). 1 new test.
